title: adiantum sulphureum var. subsulphureum
intro: Adiantum sulphureum var. subsulphureum, also known as the golden maidenhair fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Adiantaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Adiantum sulphureum var. subsulphureum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support liver function. Herbal preparations of this plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall well-being and detoxification.
